deep_dive.title: Serotonin reuptake inhibition rapidly enhances affect-reward coupling and improves mood over time
deep_dive.abstract: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs) are widely prescribed antidepressants, yet the mechanisms linking their rapid neurobiological effects to more gradual improvements in mood remain unclear. Computational accounts in which mood reflects the integration of reward outcome and reward prediction errors (RPEs) offer a compelling framework for examining SSRI effects, but reliance on laboratory tasks poses a major translational gap, both in timescale and ecological validity. We address this gap by examining the impact of SSRIs on affect-reward dynamics over a clinically relevant timescale using real-world rewards. Across 11 days, 66 healthy participants (n=37 receiving the SSRI citalopram) completed repeated ecological momentary assessments (EMA) of mood and goal-directed activities (up to 8 per day, totaling 3,542 surveys). By probing anticipated reward (pre-activity) and consummatory reward (post-activity), we derived real-world RPEs to test whether SSRIs amplify RPE impacts on momentary mood. Mixed-effects modelling showed that citalopram rapidly amplified the impact of reward outcome and RPEs on momentary mood, and predicted later increases in positive mood. Citalopram also selectively attenuated the impact of negative mood on expectations for future reward. These findings suggest that SSRIs modulate the bidirectional coupling between mood and reward processes in daily life, consistent with a positive feedback loop that could underpin the gradual mood improvement observed for antidepressant treatment. More broadly, our results highlight the utility of EMA as a highly sensitive real-world assay of early pharmacological effects on mood-reward dynamics.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Directly uses ecological momentary assessment to study affect-reward coupling and mood over time
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 直接使用生态瞬时评估研究情感-奖赏耦合及情绪随时间变化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 直接使用生态瞬时评估研究情感-奖赏耦合及情绪随时间变化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 直接使用生态瞬时评估研究情感-奖赏耦合及情绪随时间变化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Examines how SSRIs affect affect-reward dynamics using repeated ecological momentary assessments over 11 days, finding rapid enhancement of coupling and mood improvement.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究利用生态瞬时评估（EMA）对66名参与者进行为期11天的重复测量，探讨SSRI抗抑郁药西酞普兰对真实世界奖赏处理中情感-奖赏动态的影响。结果发现药物快速增强了情感与奖赏的耦合，并伴随情绪改善，弥合了实验室研究与临床实践之间的生态效度差距。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究利用生态瞬时评估（EMA）对66名参与者进行为期11天的重复测量，探讨SSRI抗抑郁药西酞普兰对真实世界奖赏处理中情感-奖赏动态的影响。结果发现药物快速增强了情感与奖赏的耦合，并伴随情绪改善，弥合了实验室研究与临床实践之间的生态效度差距。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Ecological Momentary Assessment mental health research

deep_dive.title: Night-to-night sleep EEG variability over one year
deep_dive.abstract: This study aimed to explore night-to-night variability of multiscale sleep patterns by analyzing subcutaneous electroencephalography (sqEEG) from 20 healthy participants over one year (205-388 nights per participant, 6,429 nights in total). We utilized the time series of aperiodic slopes, sigma and slow-wave power as a new whole-night unit of sleep macrostructure. Using dynamic time warping, we calculated the distances (differences) between those time series to assess night-to-night sleep macrostructure dissimilarity. We found that the overall sleep macrostructural patterns were relatively similar across nights (20% dissimilarity), while their temporal alignment was quite variable (time series warped by ~60% for the best alignment). Lower variation in macrostructure dissimilarity was associated with better subjective sleep quality (r=-0.25). Then, we qualitatively compared yearlong variation in macroscale, microscale (sleep stage proportions, mean spectral power) and mesoscale (sleep cycle duration) metrics. We found that intra-individual night-to-night variation was '"low (coefficients of variation < 20%) for spectral power, sleep duration, N2 and REM sleep; ''medium'' (20-40%) - for N3 and macrostructure dissimilarity; and "high" (>40%) - for sleep cycle duration, wake and N1. In summary, different sleep metrics showed differential night-to-night variability, which was more metric-specific than scale-dependent. This might reflect a distinction between more trait-like versus more dynamically varying features of sleep, although this assumption needs further clarification.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: sleep EEG variability analysis provides objective sleep quality measurement
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 睡眠脑电变异分析提供客观睡眠质量测量
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 睡眠脑电变异分析提供客观睡眠质量测量
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 睡眠脑电变异分析提供客观睡眠质量测量
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Analyzes night-to-night sleep EEG variability in 20 healthy participants over one year using subcutaneous EEG, revealing stable macrostructure patterns but variable temporal alignment.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 长期睡眠脑电变异性的研究匮乏。本文利用皮下脑电（sqEEG）记录20名健康被试一年（6429晚）的睡眠，采用非周期性斜率、sigma和慢波功率时间序列作为全夜睡眠宏观结构单元，通过动态时间规整评估夜间差异。发现整体宏观结构相对稳定（差异约20%），但时间对齐高度可变（扭曲约60%），为睡眠质量评估提供新指标。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 长期睡眠脑电变异性的研究匮乏。本文利用皮下脑电（sqEEG）记录20名健康被试一年（6429晚）的睡眠，采用非周期性斜率、sigma和慢波功率时间序列作为全夜睡眠宏观结构单元，通过动态时间规整评估夜间差异。发现整体宏观结构相对稳定（差异约20%），但时间对齐高度可变（扭曲约60%），为睡眠质量评估提供新指标。

deep_dive.title: Lightweight ML-Based Automatic Sleep Staging Framework with Constrained CNN and Mamba for Small-Sample EEG Datasets
deep_dive.abstract: Automatic sleep staging is a key technology for precise diagnosis and treatment of sleep disorders as well as long-term home sleep monitoring. Portable electroencephalogram (EEG) devices have become the focus of research due to their convenience in data collection. However, current methods still face three major challenges: large parameter sizes that easily lead to overfitting on small datasets, low accuracy in classifying difficult stages such as N1 and REM, unclear optimal training dataset size, and difficulty in deployment. This paper proposes GamSleepNet, a lightweight and low-latency automatic sleep staging framework for single-channel EEG. The framework features the FEB module, which combines improved Gabor kernels with learnable filters for feature extraction, uses the Mamba architecture to build a temporal classification network, introduces a novel contrastive loss and a two-stage training strategy, and experimentally validates the optimal dataset size for single-channel EEG sleep staging models. On the Sleepedf dataset, this model achieves an overall accuracy of 87.86 percent with only 30.86 thousand parameters, with all metrics reaching SOTA levels and significantly improving the identification accuracy of challenging sleep stages.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: automatic sleep staging for sleep quality assessment and measurement
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自动睡眠分期用于睡眠质量评估与测量
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自动睡眠分期用于睡眠质量评估与测量
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自动睡眠分期用于睡眠质量评估与测量
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes GamSleepNet, a lightweight automatic sleep staging framework for single-channel EEG, enabling accurate sleep stage classification on small datasets suitable for home monitoring.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有自动睡眠分期方法在小型数据集上易过拟合、困难睡眠阶段分类准确率低的问题，提出了GamSleepNet轻量级框架。该框架结合了改进的卷积神经网络和Mamba模块，通过FEB模块提取特征，在单通道EEG数据集上实现了高精度睡眠分期，尤其改善了N1和REM阶段的识别。该工作为便携式家庭睡眠监测和睡眠障碍诊断提供了高效可行的方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现有自动睡眠分期方法在小型数据集上易过拟合、困难睡眠阶段分类准确率低的问题，提出了GamSleepNet轻量级框架。该框架结合了改进的卷积神经网络和Mamba模块，通过FEB模块提取特征，在单通道EEG数据集上实现了高精度睡眠分期，尤其改善了N1和REM阶段的识别。该工作为便携式家庭睡眠监测和睡眠障碍诊断提供了高效可行的方案。

quick_skim.title: Can Conversational Temporal Dynamics Improve Depression Detection in Dyads? A Preliminary Investigation in Multi-Modality Perspectives
quick_skim.abstract: Automatic depression detection from clinical interviews typically models the semantic content and acoustic characteristics of participant speech. However, the interactional timing between the clinician and participant remains comparatively under-modeled. We investigate conversational temporal dynamics, specifically dyadic turn-pair timing, as a primary modality fused with self-supervised encoders. Evaluated on the DAIC-WOZ dataset, we compare a compact 24-dimensional timing module against frozen WavLM-large and RoBERTa-large baseline detectors. This temporal module achieves the highest single-modality performance on the development set. Furthermore, a convex-weighted late fusion strategy improves overall performance to 0.804 and 0.669 macro-F1 on the development and test sets, respectively. The learned fusion effectively assigns zero weight to acoustics, demonstrating that conversational timing serves as a lightweight, interpretable complement for dyadic depression screening.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: depression detection from conversational dynamics aligns with depression mood assessment requirement
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用对话时间动态进行抑郁检测，符合抑郁情绪评估的需求
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用对话时间动态进行抑郁检测，符合抑郁情绪评估的需求
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用对话时间动态进行抑郁检测，符合抑郁情绪评估的需求
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes using dyadic turn-pair timing as a modality for automatic depression detection from clinical interviews; achieves competitive performance and shows promise for mood assessment.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有抑郁检测多依赖语义和声学特征，忽略了人际互动时间结构。本文提出利用医患对话中轮对时序特征（24维时间模块）进行抑郁检测，在DAIC-WOZ数据集上取得与WavLM-large和RoBERTa-large基线相当的开发集表现，表明对话时间动态可作为抑郁评估的有效新模态。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有抑郁检测多依赖语义和声学特征，忽略了人际互动时间结构。本文提出利用医患对话中轮对时序特征（24维时间模块）进行抑郁检测，在DAIC-WOZ数据集上取得与WavLM-large和RoBERTa-large基线相当的开发集表现，表明对话时间动态可作为抑郁评估的有效新模态。
